explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in B tier with an overall score of 33 from a base nutrition score of 33. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: helps meals actually work
topPros.explanation: A fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one.

topPros.explanation: That can be a strength in the right context.

topPros.explanation: That leverage matters when the fat quality is good.

topCons.title: extremely calorie dense by default
topCons.explanation: That is the unavoidable category burden.

topCons.title: not a complete food on its own
topCons.explanation: Even the better fats are still just part of a meal.

closing.summary: Palm Oil does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
